story.i_want: Implement conversation accumulation pattern for retry logic when Devin session resumption is not viable
story.so_that: Failed verification steps can be retried with full conversation context, allowing agents to see and fix previous errors

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each step execution creates a node-specific directory
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a saga step execution, When the step completes, Then a node-specific directory is created at .sagas/<saga-id>/<node-name>/attempt_<N>/

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Three files captured per attempt: input.txt, output.txt, verification.txt
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step execution, When state is captured, Then input.txt, output.txt, and verification.txt are written to the attempt directory

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Retry attempts compose accumulated conversation history
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a failed verification, When a retry is triggered, Then the new prompt contains original input + all previous outputs + all previous verification feedback

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Each retry creates a new Devin agent (no session resumption)
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a retry attempt, When the orchestrator executes the step, Then a new Devin agent is created (not resuming a previous session)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Retry limit enforced via existing traversal limit mechanism
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a step with retry attempts, When the traversal limit is reached, Then no further retries are attempted

acceptance_criteria.criterion: File naming is clear and self-documenting
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given state files, When examined, Then file names (input.txt, output.txt, verification.txt) clearly indicate their purpose

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Existing saga execution flow preserved (no breaking changes)
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given existing sagas, When executed with new code, Then they continue to work without modification

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Logs clearly show retry attempts and accumulated prompt sizes
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a retry attempt, When logs are examined, Then they show attempt number and accumulated prompt size

edge_cases.criterion: First attempt (no previous attempts to accumulate)
edge_cases.gherkin: Given a step execution on first attempt, When state is captured, Then input.txt contains original prompt only
Given accumulated prompt exceeds token limits, When retry is attempted, Then agent returns error and verification fails
Given verification script runs successfully with no output, When state is captured, Then verification.txt is empty or contains success indicator

edge_cases.criterion: Node directory already exists from previous saga run
edge_cases.gherkin: Given node directory exists from previous saga, When new saga runs same node, Then new attempt subdirectory is created with incremented number
Given attempt subdirectory exists but files are incomplete, When retry logic checks for next attempt, Then next attempt number is correctly determined

edge_cases.criterion: Multiple retries with growing prompt size
edge_cases.gherkin: Given 5+ retry attempts, When accumulated prompt is composed, Then all previous outputs and verification feedback are included

orchestrator: Orchestrator._write_session_state() - Modify to create node directories and write three files
Orchestrator._determine_attempt_number() - New method to discover next attempt number
Orchestrator.compose_retry_prompt() - New method to load previous attempts and build accumulated prompt
saga_executor: SagaExecutor._execute_step() - Modify to detect retries and call compose_retry_prompt()
SagaExecutor._execute_node() - Modify to pass saga context to orchestrator
devin_wrapper: DevinWrapper.execute_prompt() - No changes needed (already handles new prompts)

steps.description: Phase 1: Modify Orchestrator._write_session_state() to create node-specific directories and write three state files (input.txt, output.txt, verification.txt)
Phase 1: Implement Orchestrator._determine_attempt_number() to scan existing attempt directories and return next attempt number
Phase 2: Implement Orchestrator.compose_retry_prompt() to load previous attempts from node directory and build accumulated conversation
Phase 2: Modify SagaExecutor._execute_step() to detect retries by checking for existing attempt directories
Phase 2: Modify SagaExecutor._execute_step() to call compose_retry_prompt() when retry is detected
Phase 3: Modify SagaExecutor._execute_node() to pass saga_id, node_name, and attempt_number to orchestrator
Phase 3: Update Orchestrator.invoke_step() signature to accept saga_id, node_name, and attempt_number parameters
Phase 3: Add logging for retry attempts and accumulated prompt sizes to SagaExecutor
Phase 4 (Optional): Remove deprecated session ID tracking code from Orchestrator

unit_tests.description: Test _determine_attempt_number() returns 1 for new node directory
Test _determine_attempt_number() returns N+1 for existing attempts
Test compose_retry_prompt() loads previous attempts correctly
Test compose_retry_prompt() builds accumulated prompt with correct format
Test _write_session_state() writes three files (input.txt, output.txt, verification.txt)
Test _execute_step() detects retry by checking for existing attempts

integration_tests.description: Test full retry flow: initial execution -> verification failure -> retry with accumulated prompt
Test multiple retries with growing accumulated prompt
Test retry limit enforcement via traversal limit
Test backward compatibility: existing sagas work without modification
Test state file structure is created correctly across retries

e2e_tests.description: Test saga with verification failure retries successfully with accumulated context
Test saga respects traversal limit and exits after max retries
Test attempt directories and files are preserved for debugging

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Accumulated prompt size grows linearly with retry attempts and may exceed token limits
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Log accumulated prompt size at each retry. Let agent fail naturally if token limit exceeded. Future enhancement: implement prompt compression if needed.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Directory structure changes could break existing saga state files
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Implement backward compatibility: support both old (.process/<step>/) and new (.sagas/<saga-id>/<node-name>/attempt_<N>/) structures. Gradual migration.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Partial directory creation during crash could leave inconsistent state
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Implement robust attempt number detection that handles incomplete directories. Use atomic file writes where possible.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Verification script output format may vary, making parsing unreliable
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Store raw verification output in verification.txt. No parsing required. Agent sees full context.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Storage grows unbounded with retry attempts and saga executions
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Preserve all attempts for debugging (as required). Implement optional cleanup on saga completion (future enhancement).

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Retry detection logic could miss attempts due to directory scanning issues
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Implement robust directory scanning with error handling. Test edge cases (missing directories, permission issues, etc.).
